title.zh: 破解蛋白質遺傳密碼：最大規模蛋白質基因組學研究揭示疾病治療新方向
title.en: Decoding the Protein Genetic Code: Largest Proteogenomic Study Reveals New Directions for Disease Treatment

summary.zh:
【總結】
這項研究整合了38個大型研究的78,664名參與者的基因與蛋白質數據，創下了蛋白質基因組學研究規模之最。研究人員鑑定出超過24,000個蛋白質數量性狀位點(pQTLs)，涉及1,116種蛋白質，揭示了基因如何影響蛋白質表達的複雜網絡。通過機器學習分析，他們發現N-連接糖基化是一個關鍵的調節途徑，並證明了「順式」(cis)與「反式」(trans)遺傳調節在表型表現上的差異。最重要的是，研究為藥物重定位提供了寶貴線索，例如發現酪氨酸激酶2(TYK2)抑制劑可能治療類風濕性關節炎。

【研究問題】
過去的研究多關注基因變異與單一蛋白質的關聯，卻忽視了基因對整個蛋白質組(proteome)的系統性影響。同時，傳統觀察性研究難以確定蛋白質與疾病之間的因果關係。本研究旨在回答：遺傳變異如何影響循環蛋白質水平？不同類型的遺傳調節(順式vs反式)會產生什麼不同的表型後果？如何利用這些遺傳發現來識別新的藥物靶點和重定位機會？

【研究方法】
研究團隊採用了多隊列(meta-analysis)方法，整合了來自38個獨立研究的78,664名參與者的基因組與蛋白質組數據。他們首先進行了全基因組關聯分析(GWAS)鑑定蛋白質數量性狀位點(pQTLs)，然後應用機器學習技術分配效應基因，分析調節途徑、細胞類型和組織貢獻。為確保結果可靠性，研究人員進行了精細定位(colocalization)分析，並比較了順式與反式遺傳調節的表型後果。最後，他們通過系統性三角測量方法，將反式-pQTLs與遺傳和蛋白質聯繫起來，尋找藥物重定位機會。

【結果】
研究鑑定出超過24,000個pQTLs，其中5,040個為順式pQTLs，19,698個為反式pQTLs，涉及1,116種蛋白質。這些遺傳變異解釋了蛋白質水平變異性的約5-40%。機器學習分析顯示，N-連接糖基化是調節循環蛋白質水平的重要途徑。有趣的是，「順式」與「反式」遺傳調節揭示了不同的表型聯繫：順式調節主要影響蛋白質生產相關表型，而反式調節則影響更廣泛的表型。研究發現，血漿furin蛋白與心血管疾病相關，而TYK2抑制劑可能成為類風濕性關節炎的新治療選擇。此外，研究發現基於遺傳的藥物重定位比傳統觀察性研究更可靠，為精準醫療提供了新方向。
summary.en:
**Summary:**
This landmark study represents the largest proteogenomic investigation to date, integrating genomic and proteomic data from 78,664 participants across 38 cohorts. The researchers identified over 24,000 protein quantitative trait loci (QTLs) associated with 1,116 proteins, revealing the complex genetic architecture underlying protein levels in circulation. Through machine learning-guided effector gene assignment, they demonstrated that N-linked glycosylation represents a critical regulatory pathway for plasma proteins. Critically, the research distinguishes between "cis" (local) and "trans" (distant) genetic regulation, showing how these different mechanisms produce distinct phenotypic consequences. The findings provide valuable insights for drug target identification and repurposing, with particular emphasis on TYK2 inhibitors for rheumatoid arthritis treatment, highlighting the power of proteogenomic approaches for understanding disease mechanisms and therapeutic opportunities.

**Problem:**
Previous research has largely focused on individual gene-protein relationships without systematically addressing how genetic variation affects the entire proteome simultaneously. Observational studies have struggled to establish causal relationships between protein levels and diseases, often confounded by environmental factors and reverse causality. This study addresses three fundamental questions: 1) How do genetic variants systematically influence circulating protein levels across the proteome? 2) How do cis- versus trans-acting genetic mechanisms produce different phenotypic outcomes? 3) How can these genetic insights be leveraged to identify novel drug targets and repurposing opportunities with improved causal inference?

**Approach:**
The researchers employed a multi-cohort meta-analysis framework, combining genomic and proteomic data from 38 independent studies totaling 78,664 participants. They conducted genome-wide association studies (GWAS) to identify protein quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) and applied machine learning techniques to assign effector genes and identify enriched pathways, cell types, and tissues. Fine-mapping and colocalization analyses ensured robust variant identification. The study then contrasted cis-focused versus trans-focused genetic instruments to reveal distinct phenotypic associations. Finally, they systematically triangulated trans-pQTLs with genetic and protein associations across multiple diseases using Mendelian randomization and enrichment analyses to identify potential drug repurposing opportunities.

**Results:**
The study identified over 24,000 pQTLs (5,040 cis-pQTLs and 19,698 trans-pQTLs) affecting 1,116 proteins, with these genetic variants explaining approximately 5-40% of protein level variability. Machine learning analysis highlighted N-linked glycosylation as a critical regulatory pathway for plasma proteins. Notably, cis- and trans-acting genetic mechanisms revealed distinct phenotypic associations: cis-regulation primarily affected protein production-related phenotypes, while trans-regulation influenced broader phenotypic landscapes. The research identified plasma furin as a potential drug target for cardiovascular diseases and suggested TYK2 inhibitors as candidates for repurposing in rheumatoid arthritis treatment. Crucially, genetically informed approaches demonstrated higher concordance with causal effects compared to observational studies, providing more reliable targets for therapeutic intervention.

keyArticle.title: Multi-cohort proteogenomic analyses reveal genetic effects across the proteome and diseasome
